response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile full-stack engineer with strong web, Node.js/TypeScript, and DLang systems background, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise in industrial automation. His only professional C++ exposure was a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, C++ is absent from his skills list, and he shows no experience with MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices. Despite exceeding the years-of-experience bar and having genuine low-latency systems work (in DLang), the core technical match is too weak for a senior C++ leadership role in this domain.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months in 2014–2015); C++ not listed in current skills
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack and DLang, not the C++/STL architecture focus this role requires
